explanation:
The property describes the requirement that during ventilation, if a change to PSV mode is requested (indicated by PSVModeSelected) and the inspiratory phase of PCV ends (indicated by inspiratoryPhaseEnd), then the transition to PSV mode (i.e., PCVMode becomes false and PSVMode becomes true) should occur. 

The property is encoded using the trigger condition 'ventilating & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd' implying, at every step of the execution (globally), if during ventilation a change to PSV mode is requested and the current cycle is at the end of the inspiratory phase, then the system will be in PSV mode in the next step, i.e., PCV mode is no longer active and PSV mode is active.

N_DURATION is set to null as immediate (next-step) transitions are expected, not time-bounded behavior.

The property describes that, whenever (globally) ventilation is on (ventilating), a transition from PCV mode (PCVMode) to PSV mode (PSVMode) shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time, but only if the command to change to PSV mode has been received from the GUI (PSVModeSelected). The relevant event marking the end of the inspiratory time in PCV is inspiratoryPhaseEnd. Thus, at every moment: if we are ventilating, in PCV mode, and the user has commanded a switch to PSV, then at the end of the inspiratory phase, PSVMode should become true (i.e., mode should switch). This is a conditional requirement with multiple conditions, so we use a template that allows us to conjunct multiple requirements in the antecedent. We choose the template: (G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3))), where bool_exp1 = ventilating, bool_exp2 = (PCVMode & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and bool_exp3 = PSVMode. This states: whenever the system is ventilating and the preceding conditions hold and the inspiratory phase of PCV ends, in the next step the ventilator should be in PSV mode.

The requirement is that the transition from PCV to PSV mode happens at the end of the PCV inspiratory phase (which is marked by 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd'), but only if ventilation is active ('ventilating') and the change mode command to PSV has been received ('PSVModeSelected'). This means that whenever all the following hold at the same time: ventilation is on, change to PSV mode command has been received, and the inspiratory phase ends, then a transition to PSV mode (represented by 'PSVMode') must follow immediately. This is captured by the template: G((ventilating & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd) -> (X PSVMode)), meaning at all times, if the ventilation is on, the user has selected PSV mode, and the inspiratory phase ends, then in the next time step, the mode should be PSV.

The property expresses that when the system is ventilating (ventilation is on), and a transition to PSV mode is commanded (PSVModeSelected) at the end of the PCV inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) while the mode is currently PCV (PCVMode), then a transition to PSV mode (PSVMode) will occur in the next step. This aligns with the requirement that the switch from PCV to PSV only happens if ventilation is on, at the end of a PCV inspiration, and the GUI has requested the mode change.

The property describes the required transition from PCV to PSV mode in a ventilator. According to the natural language, this transition must happen if (1) ventilation is on, (2) the transition command to PSV mode has been given from the GUI (PSVModeSelected), and (3) the inspiratory phase of the PCV mode ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), OR more strictly, at the conclusion of a PCV inspiratory time. Thus, the LTL formula must express that whenever ventilating AND PSVModeSelected AND inspiratoryPhaseEnd hold (i.e., all three conditions at the same time), then in the next step, PSVMode must hold (i.e., system must switch to PSV mode right after). This is best captured using the template: G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2 & bool_exp3) -> (X bool_exp4)), where the conjunction of required inputs implies a transition to the PSV mode in the next state. Although the language asks specifically "at the end of a PCV inspiratory time", the closest proposition to encode that from the available list is "inspiratoryPhaseEnd" (indicating exactly that event). N_DURATION is not relevant for this formula as it's an immediate next-step property.

The natural language states that when in PCV mode (as ventilation is on), a transition to PSV mode should only occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time, and only if the GUI has issued a mode change command (to PSV). Mapping this: 1) 'ventilating' denotes ventilation is on, 2) 'PCVMode' denotes that the machine is in PCV mode for this transition, 3) 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' denotes the end of the inspiratory phase (serving as a proxy for end of PCV inspiratory time), 4) 'PSVModeSelected' denotes the explicit change command from the GUI to PSV mode. The LTL property should state that globally, whenever the system is ventilating, in PCVMode, at the end of the inspiratory phase, and the change-to-PSV command has been issued, then (in the next step) the mode should actually switch to PSVMode. We want to specify this temporal requirement, so choosing template 41: G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)), where bool_exp1 = ventilating, bool_exp2 = (PCVMode & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected), and bool_exp3 = PSVMode (indicating the PSV mode is reached in the next step after the above conjunction). N_DURATION is not used for this template.

The property requires that whenever ventilation is on (represented by 'ventilating'), if at the end of a PCV inspiratory phase (represented by 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd') and the mode change command to PSV was issued by the GUI (represented by 'PSVModeSelected'), then a transition to PSV mode (represented by 'PSVMode') should occur in the next state. This matches the requirement that the transition from PCV to PSV should occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the command has been received and ventilation is active.

The English requirement specifies: when ventilating (ventilating is ON), and the change mode command to PSV mode has been given by the GUI (PSVModeSelected), the system shall switch from PCV mode to PSV at the end of a PCV inspiratory phase (end of inspiratory phase in PCV mode). To capture this, we need a temporal logic property that ensures: Whenever (ventilation is on) AND (PSV switch command is received) AND (we are in PCV mode), then at the END OF the current inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), PSV mode is selected. This is best captured using a template that expresses, whenever a condition holds (i.e., all the above conditions), at the next end of the PCV inspiratory phase, PSV mode starts. To encode this, I'll use: G((ventilating & PCVMode & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd) -> X PSVMode), which fits template 11 (G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3))).

Here, bool_exp2 = ventilating & PCVMode & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd
bool_exp3 = PSVMode
N_DURATION is irrelevant for this template (set to null).

This LTL formula expresses that while ventilation is active (ventilating), whenever PSV mode (PSVMode) should be selected, it must occur immediately following the end of the PCV inspiratory phase, and only if the PSV mode selection command (PSVModeSelected) has been received. 

Formally, this is captured by: Whenever (ventilating & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd) is true (at the end of a PCV inspiratory time, command from GUI is received, and ventilation is active), the next state should have PSV mode active (PSVMode). This captures the requirement in the natural language that the transition from PCV to PSV only happens under these joint conditions and at the correct time (end of PCV inspiration), and packs everything into the response-to-event pattern using the appropriate connectivity of atomic propositions.

This formalization uses the template: G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)). Here, bool_exp1 (ventilating) captures that ventilation is on, bool_exp2 (PCVMode & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd) encodes that the system is in PCV mode, the change mode command to PSV has been received, and the PCV inspiratory phase is ending. The right side, (X PSVMode), states that in the next state, the system transitions into PSV mode. This matches the requirement that, while ventilation is ongoing, a transition from PCV to PSV should happen at the end of inspiratory phase after the command from GUI is received.
